title: Garlicky Aerated Canadian Halibut Poke
description: A show-off Canadian dish featuring dark brown sugar and sashimi-cut halibut, aerated for a light and refreshing twist on traditional poke. Ideal for a Mediterranean-inspired dinner.
instructions: Rinse the halibut fillets under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Using a mandoline or sharp knife, slice the halibut into thin sashimi pieces.
In a blender or food processor, combine the garlic, dark brown sugar, soy sauce, rice vinegar, ginger, and green onions.
Blend the mixture until it is smooth and well combined.
With the blender or food processor running, slowly pour in the avocado oil through the top.
Continue blending until the mixture is well aerated and has a light and fluffy texture.
Season the aerated mixture with sea salt and black pepper to taste.
Add the sashimi-cut halibut to the aerated mixture and gently fold until it is well coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the poke a gentle stir and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the Garlicky Aerated Canadian Halibut Poke chilled, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
